content: Nginx WebSocket/Socket.io reverse proxy requires: proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade" (via a map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade block, since Connection is hop-by-hop and not forwarded by default); proxy_set_header Host $host; X-Real-IP/X-Forwarded-For/X-Forwarded-Proto for correct client IP/protocol visibility to the app; proxy_read_timeout and proxy_send_timeout raised to a large value (e.g. 86400s) because default nginx timeouts (60s) will kill idle-but-open long-poll/websocket connections; proxy_buffering off recommended for real-time responsiveness. If ever running more than one Node process behind nginx, sticky sessions (ip_hash or Socket.io's own sticky-session helpers) are required because Socket.io's default HTTP long-polling handshake needs the same server for a given client across requests — not a concern for this project's single-process single-VPS deployment but worth noting if PM2 cluster mode is ever used.
